title: Amman 7-Day Heritage Tour
metaTitle: Amman 7-Day Heritage Tour | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 7-day trip to Amman, Jordan: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Petra Archaeological Park and Jerash Roman ruins. Prices from $1591 per person (estimate).
highlights: Multi-day Petra hiking trails (High Place of Sacrifice, Monastery trail)
overview: Spend 7 days discovering Amman, Jordan — Semi-arid climate — hot, dry summers (highs around 32-33°C) and cool, sometimes wet winters (highs around 12°C, occasional snow in Amman). March-May and September-November, for mild temperatures ideal for exploring Petra and desert sites on foot. This itinerary blends Petra Archaeological Park, Jerash Roman ruins, Rainbow Street with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
6 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Petra Archaeological Park, Jerash Roman ruins, Rainbow Street
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Amman
itinerary.description: Land at Queen Alia International Airport (AMM) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Petra Archaeological Park — UNESCO World Heritage rock-cut city, including the Treasury and Monastery. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Amman: Jerash Roman ruins & Wadi Rum desert jeep tour and overnight camp
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Jerash Roman ruins — One of the best-preserved Roman provincial cities outside Italy. In the afternoon, continue to Wadi Rum desert jeep tour and overnight camp — Mars-like red desert landscape with Bedouin-run camps. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Wadi Rum desert jeep tour and overnight camp

itinerary.title: Amman: Dead Sea float and mud spa & Amman Citadel
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Dead Sea float and mud spa — Buoyant swim and mineral-mud treatment at a Dead Sea resort. In the afternoon, continue to Amman Citadel — Hilltop ruins overlooking the city, including a Roman temple and Umayyad palace. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Dead Sea float and mud spa

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Petra
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Petra, a popular side trip from Amman. Return to Amman in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Petra by Night candlelit walk to the Treasury — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.
activities: Petra by Night candlelit walk to the Treasury

itinerary.title: Amman: Rainbow Street & Roman Theatre, Amman
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Rainbow Street — Amman's lively café- and gallery-lined pedestrian street. In the afternoon, continue to Roman Theatre, Amman — 2nd-century amphitheater in the city center. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Downtown Amman souqs or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to AMM for your departure flight.

travelTips: Buy the Jordan Pass online before arrival — it bundles Petra entry (multi-day), other site fees, and often waives the visa fee.
Allow a full day (or two) for Petra — the site is vast and the walk to the Monastery alone takes hours round-trip.
Book a Wadi Rum overnight camp for one of the best stargazing experiences available anywhere.
Float, don't swim, at the Dead Sea, and avoid getting the water in your eyes.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Sturdy hiking shoes for Petra's uneven stone paths and stairs
Modest clothing for religious and rural sites
A swimsuit for the Dead Sea
Layers for cool desert nights in Wadi Rum
thingsToDo: Wadi Rum desert jeep tour and overnight camp
Dead Sea float and mud spa
Multi-day Petra hiking trails (High Place of Sacrifice, Monastery trail)
Dead Sea float and beach resorts
thingsToAvoid: Rushing Petra in a few hours — the site rewards a full day, ideally two.
Shaving before a Dead Sea visit — the mineral-rich water stings broken skin.
Bargaining aggressively in fixed-price modern shops (only expected in traditional souqs).
Underestimating Petra's walking distances and heat — bring more water than you think you need.
topRestaurants: Hashem Restaurant — Falafel and hummus institution ($)
Beit Sitti — Home-style Jordanian cooking classes ($$$)
instagramSpots: Petra's Treasury framed by the Siq canyon
Wadi Rum red desert dunes at sunset
hiddenGems: Petra by Night candlelit walk to the Treasury
familyActivities: Dead Sea float and beach resorts
adventureActivities: Multi-day Petra hiking trails (High Place of Sacrifice, Monastery trail)
luxuryExperiences: Private guided Petra tour with skip-the-line access
Luxury desert camp in Wadi Rum with stargazing
Five-star Dead Sea resort spa stay
honeymoonExperiences: Petra by Night candlelit visit for two
Private overnight luxury camp in Wadi Rum
budgetExperiences: Free wander through Rainbow Street and Downtown souqs
Amman Citadel and Roman Theatre (low-cost entry)
Public Dead Sea beach access instead of a resort

festivalCalendar.name: Jerash Festival of Culture and Arts

localEtiquette: Dress modestly, particularly outside Amman's more cosmopolitan districts and at religious sites.
Hospitality is central to Jordanian culture — accepting offered tea or coffee is polite.
Use your right hand for eating and giving/receiving items.
Bargaining is expected in souqs but not in fixed-price shops.
localTransportGuide: Amman itself has limited public transit for visitors — taxis (metered or app-based via Careem) are the easiest way around the city. For Petra, Wadi Rum, and the Dead Sea, most travelers book a private driver or join a multi-day tour, since sites are spread across the country with no direct rail link.
bestTimeToVisit: March-May and September-November, for mild temperatures ideal for exploring Petra and desert sites on foot.
weather: Semi-arid climate — hot, dry summers (highs around 32-33°C) and cool, sometimes wet winters (highs around 12°C, occasional snow in Amman).
visaInformation: Most nationalities, including the US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, and India, can obtain a visa on arrival or e-visa; travelers booking through a licensed tour operator for 2+ nights often qualify for a free "Jordan Pass" visa waiver that also bundles Petra entry.
averageFlightTime: ~9.5h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Amman?
faqs.answer: Most nationalities, including the US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, and India, can obtain a visa on arrival or e-visa; travelers booking through a licensed tour operator for 2+ nights often qualify for a free "Jordan Pass" visa waiver that also bundles Petra entry.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Amman?
faqs.answer: March-May and September-November, for mild temperatures ideal for exploring Petra and desert sites on foot. Semi-arid climate — hot, dry summers (highs around 32-33°C) and cool, sometimes wet winters (highs around 12°C, occasional snow in Amman).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Amman safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Amman is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Amman?
faqs.answer: The local currency is JOD. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Wei Nguyen
reviews.title: Five stars, will return
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Amman Citadel in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: James Novak
reviews.title: Great value for money
reviews.body: Our trip to Amman was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Amman Citadel was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Sarah Williams
reviews.title: Great value for money
reviews.body: Amman is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Downtown Amman souqs. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.
